title: Massachusetts law about disability rights in housing
content:
Massachusetts law about disability rights in housing

Laws, regulations, and web & print sources on disability rights in housing.

Key Massachusetts Laws:
- Article CXIV of the Massachusetts Constitution: "No otherwise qualified handicapped individual shall, solely by reason of his handicap, be excluded from the participation in, denied the benefits of, or be subject to discrimination under any program or activity within the commonwealth."
- MGL c. 93, s. 103 - Equal rights; age and handicap; violations; remedies (Mass. Equal Rights Act - MERA)
- MGL c. 151B - Unlawful discrimination: s.1 Definitions (handicap, qualified handicapped person), s.4 Unlawful practices, s.4(3B) Prohibits discrimination in mortgage lending, s.4(6) and (7) Prohibits housing discrimination (renting, buying & selling), s.4(7A) Reasonable modifications to housing, s.4(10) Prohibits discrimination against those who receive government assistance
- MGL c. 12, s. 11I - Violations of constitutional rights; civil actions by aggrieved persons
- MGL c. 40A, s. 3 - Local land use and health and safety laws shall not discriminate against a disabled person
- MGL c. 121B - Housing and urban renewal: s.39 Power to provide housing for elderly and handicapped persons, s.40 Provisions applicable to housing for elderly and handicapped persons
- MGL c. 188, s. 2 - Homestead protection for elderly or disabled persons

Key Massachusetts Regulations:
- 521 CMR 9.00 - Multiple dwellings accessibility standards (buildings with 3+ dwelling units)
- 760 CMR 5.00 - Eligibility and selection criteria (State-aided housing), s.5.07 Eligibility of handicapped persons
- 804 CMR 2.00 - Discrimination in housing (MCAD), s.2.03 Housing discrimination on the basis of handicap

Key Federal Laws:
- Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 (29 U.S.C. s. 794) - protects individuals with disabilities from discrimination by entities receiving federal funds
- Americans with Disabilities Act (42 U.S.C. ss. 12131-12133) - applies to public housing authorities
- Federal Fair Housing Act (42 U.S.C. ss. 3601 et seq.) - prohibits housing discrimination based on disability

Filing Complaints:
- Massachusetts Commission Against Discrimination (MCAD): (617) 994-6000
- Massachusetts Attorney General's Civil Rights Division: (617) 963-2917
- Boston Regional Office of Fair Housing and Equal Opportunity (HUD): (617) 994-8300

Key Resources:
- Tenancy Preservation Program, Mass. Housing Court: homelessness prevention for disability-related eviction issues
- Tenants' guide to eviction: elderly or disabled tenants can request a stay of execution up to a year
